research_findings: The current loop can persist review_state, but write-report.py and publish-iteration.py still treat that artifact as optional, so a version can slip through without a matching recorded review.
The right gate condition is goal-aware: a captured review_state must exist and match the active goal or draft goal when committee review is required by config.
validate-kit should exercise the failure mode directly, otherwise the new gate would live only in documentation and source inspection.
committee_feedback: Product manager committee wants committee review to become an actual release gate, not a best-effort habit that can be skipped under pressure.
Technical architect committee wants one shared helper for review-state enforcement so report and publish reject the same missing-or-stale conditions.
User committee wants clear failure messages that say to run capture-review.py instead of silently producing incomplete reports.
committee_decision: Add a shared require_review_state helper, call it from write-report.py and publish-iteration.py, and make validate-kit assert that report and publish fail when matching review_state is absent.
review_state.goal_title: Require recorded review state before reporting and publication

results.name: validate-kit

history.goal: Add a real repository validation script and use it as the publish gate
Fix publish ordering so loop state and backlog updates are committed in the same version
Add a reference that defines what high-quality project data means for the loop
Add a structured schema for project data snapshots and evidence
Add a script that collects first-pass project data into a snapshot file
Add a script that scores whether the collected project data is good enough to act on
Integrate data snapshot and quality state into the loop's durable state model
Extend reports to include evidence sources and data quality observations
Improve next-goal selection using data quality gaps and evidence gaps
Align wrappers, protocol, README, and examples around high-quality project data acquisition
Integrate the committee-driven loop model across config, scripts, templates, and references
Persist research and committee review conclusions into loop state
Read committee review state back into project data and reports
Add a project installation script for the committee-driven kit
Require recorded review state before reporting and publication
